authors: Kon, Liu, Qiu, Fan, He, Lin, Zhang, Park, Elengikal, Kang, Chen, Chowdhury, Lee, Wang
title: IaC-Eval: A Code Generation Benchmark for Cloud Infrastructure-as-Code Programs
key_findings: GPT-4 achieves 19.36% pass@1 vs 86.6% on EvalPlus. Foundational benchmark establishing IaC generation gap.
notes: Foundational benchmark. GitHub: autoiac-project/iac-eval. NeurIPS D&B track.

title: TerraFormer: Automated IaC with LLMs Fine-Tuned via Policy-Guided Verifier Feedback
model_types: Sonnet 3.7, DeepSeek-R1, GPT-4.1, CodeLlama, custom fine-tuned
tool_use_external_verifier: yes - tflint, terraform validate, Checkov, tfsec
key_findings: +15.94% on IaC-Eval over base LLM. Outperforms 50x larger models on TF-Gen/TF-Mutn. Top security compliance. Neuro-symbolic approach.
notes: Strongest paper. Accepted ICSE 2026 SEIP. ONLY paper with explicit security compliance scoring via formal verification.

title: Multi-Agent Code-Orchestrated Generation for Reliable IaC (MACOG)
agent_architecture: Architect + Provider Harmonizer + Engineer + Reviewer + Security Prover + Cost Planner + DevOps + Memory Curator
key_findings: GPT-5: 54.90 (RAG) → 74.02 with MACOG. Includes Security Prover agent and OPA policy enforcement. Ablation shows constrained decoding and deploy feedback critical.
notes: Multi-agent with dedicated Security Prover. arXiv only.

title: IaC Generation with LLMs: An Error Taxonomy and Configuration Knowledge Injection
key_findings: Baseline 27.1% overall success. Knowledge injection: tech validation → 75.3%, overall → 62.6%. Identifies Correctness-Congruence Gap: LLMs become good coders but poor architects.
notes: Key contribution: two-dimensional error taxonomy (validation stage x failure pattern).

title: A Survey of using Large Language Models for Generating Infrastructure as Code
key_findings: Only prior survey. GPT-3.5-Turbo: 59% accuracy, CodeParrot: 8% on small 49-task benchmark. Shallow coverage.
notes: Published at ICON 2023 (small NLP conf). No systematic methodology. Useful only as baseline.

title: Multi-IaC-Eval: Benchmarking Cloud IaC Across Multiple Formats
key_findings: >95% syntactic validity across formats but significant challenges in semantic alignment and complex patterns. First multi-format IaC benchmark.
notes: Extends IaC-Eval to CloudFormation and CDK. Covers AWS only.

title: Deployability-Centric IaC Generation (IaCGen)
model_types: GPT-4o, GPT-o3-mini, Claude-3.5, Claude-3.7, DeepSeek-R1, DeepSeek-V3
tool_use_external_verifier: yes - format verification, syntax checking, live deployment
key_findings: Only 20.8% passItr@1 for deployability. IaCGen achieves 79.7% on TF. Security compliance: only 8.4%. User intent coverage: 25.2%. Critical need for security research.
notes: First deployability-centric benchmark. v3 adds security analysis. 8.4% security compliance is damning.

title: Using a Feedback Loop for LLM-based IaC Generation
key_findings: Feedback loop improves CloudFormation generation. Grammar-aligned decoding helps syntactic correctness.
notes: Early feedback loop work on CF. Single model tested.

authors: Li, Grella, Nahmias, Engelberg, Klein, Guizzardi, et al.
title: GenSIaC: Toward Security-Aware IaC Generation with LLMs
key_findings: F1: 0.303 to 0.858 after fine-tuning. Cross-language generalization works. First security-focused IaC generation study.
notes: KEY PAPER for security. Covers config mgmt IaC (Ansible/Chef/Puppet) not provisioning (TF/CF). Uses GLITCH for security weakness extraction.

title: ACSE-Eval: Can LLMs Threat Model Real-World Cloud Infrastructure?
model_types: GPT-4.1, GPT-4o, Claude-3.5 Haiku, Claude-3.7 Sonnet, DeepSeek-V3, Gemini-2.5 Pro
key_findings: GPT-4.1 and Gemini 2.5 Pro excel at threat identification. Gemini best in 0-shot, GPT-4.1 in few-shot. 100 production-grade scenarios with IaC + documented vulns.
notes: Tests LLM security reasoning ON IaC. Not generation but validation/analysis. Uses CDK TypeScript.

title: Repairing Infrastructure-as-Code using Large Language Models
key_findings: LLM for IaC misconfiguration repair. Addresses security misconfigs in Terraform. IEEE peer-reviewed.
notes: Need full-text for complete extraction. IEEE peer-reviewed.

title: An LLM-driven Framework for Dynamic IaC Generation
key_findings: RAG + code ensemble for dynamic IaC generation. Addresses static template limitations.
notes: Demo/poster paper from Middleware. Need full-text.

title: Security Smells in IaC Scripts: A Taxonomy
iac_languages: Ansible, Terraform, Chef, Puppet, Pulumi, SaltStack, Vagrant
key_findings: LLM-assisted security smell detection across 7 IaC tools. Extended taxonomy from previous studies. Human validation at each stage.
notes: Covers broadest range of IaC tools (7). Security-focused. Uses LLM as analysis assistant with human validation.

title: Cloud Infrastructure Management in the Age of AI Agents
agent_architecture: SDK agent, CLI agent, IaC agent, Web portal agent
key_findings: AI agents using 4 interfaces (SDK/CLI/IaC/Web) for cloud management. Compares Terraform agent vs other approaches. Preliminary study.
notes: Preliminary study. Tests Terraform as one of 4 agent interfaces. Azure-focused (rare).

title: Towards self-healing of IaC projects using constrained LLM
key_findings: Constrained LLM approach for IaC self-healing/repair. ICSE peer-reviewed.
notes: Need full-text. ICSE is top SE venue. Constrained generation approach.

title: Towards Formal Verification of LLM-Generated Code from NL Prompts
key_findings: Formal verification of LLM-generated Ansible code. Novel approach: verify correctness rather than just test. Notes BLEU/CodeBERTScore inadequate for IaC.
notes: Ansible-focused. Formal methods approach.

authors: Pujar, Buratti, Guo, Dupuis, Lewis, Suneja, Sood, Nalawade, Jones, Morari, Puri
title: Automated Code Generation for IT Tasks in YAML through LLMs
key_findings: Ansible Wisdom BLEU: 66.67 outperforms Codex-Davinci-002 BLEU: 50.4 (few-shot). 56.81% functional equivalency. IBM Research.
notes: IBM Research. First Ansible-specific LLM fine-tuning. Custom metrics for YAML/Ansible.

title: Artificial Intelligence for Infrastructure-as-Code: A Systematic Literature Review
iac_languages: Chef, Puppet, Ansible, Pulumi, Heat, DOML, Terraform, TOSCA, CloudFormation
key_findings: Competing SLR. 44 studies (2020-2025). Broad AI scope (not LLM-specific). Maps to 4-phase DevOps lifecycle. 22 SE papers, 5 AI, 3 networks, 2 security. Published in MDPI Electronics.
notes: Competing review. MDPI Q2 venue. Broad AI scope dilutes LLM analysis. Cutoff Oct 2025 misses 2026 papers. No quality assessment rubric. No security cross-cutting analysis. Must cite and differentiate.

title: Generative AI as an infrastructure copilot: automating IaC across the DevSecOps lifecycle
key_findings: Journal article on gen AI as IaC copilot across DevSecOps lifecycle. Published in ASE (Springer, Q1).
notes: Need full-text. Published in top SE journal. DevSecOps focus may have security content.

title: ARPaCCino: An Agentic-RAG for Policy as Code Compliance
key_findings: Agentic RAG for policy-as-code compliance checking. Directly relevant to security/compliance lens.
notes: Need full-text. Policy compliance is directly relevant to RQ5.

title: Prompt-Driven Container Orchestration
key_findings: Prompt-driven container orchestration. Tentatively included for K8s manifest generation scope.
notes: Tentative inclusion. Need full-text to confirm K8s manifest generation vs runtime orchestration.
